name: Xana
description: Xanas are enchanting water spirits from Portuguese folklore, typically associated with springs, rivers, and other freshwater sources. They are often depicted as beautiful women with long, flowing hair, sometimes described as golden or red. Xanas are believed to possess immense wealth, often guarding hidden treasures within their watery domains. These treasures may include gold, jewels, and other valuable artifacts. While generally benevolent, Xanas can be mischievous or even dangerous if angered or disrespected. They are known to lure travelers with their beauty and enchanting songs, sometimes leading them astray or even drowning them. However, they can also bestow blessings and grant wishes to those who approach them with respect and kindness. Their connection to water makes them vital figures in the agricultural and spiritual life of rural communities, embodying the life-giving and sometimes unpredictable nature of water itself. Xanas are deeply ingrained in the cultural identity of Portugal, representing the magic and mystery of the natural world.
subtitle: Enchanting water spirit of Portuguese folklore
appearance: Beautiful women with long, flowing hair (often golden or red). They may appear partially submerged in water or adorned with aquatic plants. Some accounts describe them as having fish tails or other aquatic features.
habitat: Springs, rivers, streams, lakes, and other freshwater sources in Portugal, particularly in mountainous or forested regions.
behavior: Generally benevolent but can be mischievous or dangerous if provoked. Known to lure travelers with their beauty and songs. May bestow blessings or grant wishes to those who treat them with respect. They are guardians of treasure.

keyMyths.title: The Xana's Gold
keyMyths.description: A poor farmer stumbles upon a Xana guarding a chest of gold. He treats her with respect and asks for only what he needs. The Xana, pleased with his humility, grants him a portion of the treasure, warning him not to reveal her secret. He prospers but eventually boasts of his riches, and the Xana's gift turns to stone.

keyMyths.title: The Enchanted Singer
keyMyths.description: A young man is captivated by the beautiful singing of a Xana near a river. He follows the sound, drawn deeper into the water. The Xana attempts to lure him into her underwater realm, but he remembers a charm taught to him by his grandmother. He recites the charm, breaking the Xana's enchantment and escaping her grasp.

keyMyths.title: The Xana's Blessing
keyMyths.description: A village suffers from a prolonged drought. A young woman, known for her kindness and respect for nature, offers a gift of flowers and a heartfelt prayer to the Xana of the local spring. The Xana, moved by her sincerity, causes the spring to flow again, saving the village from starvation.
keyMyths.source: Legends of the Serra da Estrela

sources.author: Teófilo Braga
